text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Gerald Hutchinson
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Male
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: Urology / GU Oncology Service
PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]
CLINICAL HISTORY: 67-year-old male with elevated PSA (87.4 ng/mL), abnormal digital rectal exam, and multiparametric MRI demonstrating PI-RADS 5 lesion with extracapsular extension and seminal vesicle involvement. Family history of prostate cancer (father, paternal uncle). Biopsy performed for tissue confirmation.

---

SPECIMEN ID: SP-XXXX-XXXX

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE]

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Transrectal ultrasound (TRUS)-guided prostate needle biopsy, systematic 12-core template (bilateral sextant protocol with additional targeted cores to MRI-visible lesion). Cores submitted in individual, labeled cassettes.

CLINICAL INDICATION: Elevated PSA 87.4 ng/mL. Multiparametric MRI demonstrating PI-RADS 5 bilateral prostate lesion, extracapsular extension, and bilateral seminal vesicle involvement suspicious for high-grade prostate cancer. Biopsy for histologic confirmation and Gleason grading.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Received in formalin are 12 individually labeled needle core biopsy specimens designated as follows: right base (A), right mid-gland (B), right apex (C), left base (D), left mid-gland (E), left apex (F), right lateral base (G), right lateral apex (H), left lateral base (I), left lateral apex (J), right seminal vesicle sampling (K), and targeted biopsy -- left base dominant lesion (L).

Cores range in length from 8 mm to 17 mm and in diameter from 1 to 1.5 mm. Each core is individually embedded in paraffin. Serial sections obtained at three levels per block. Total cores submitted: 12. Representative H&E-stained slides prepared and reviewed.

---

DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS:

ACINAR ADENOCARCINOMA OF THE PROSTATE

10 of 12 cores are positive for adenocarcinoma.

GLEASON SCORE: 4 + 5 = 9
GRADE GROUP: 5 (per 2019 WHO/ISUP grading system)

Detailed core-by-core summary:

  A. Right base -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 8 of 14 mm (57%). Perineural invasion: PRESENT.

  B. Right mid-gland -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+4=8. Involved length: 5 of 12 mm (42%).

  C. Right apex -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 11 of 13 mm (85%). Perineural invasion: PRESENT.

  D. Left base -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 15 of 16 mm (94%). Perineural invasion: PRESENT.

  E. Left mid-gland -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 13 of 14 mm (93%). Perineural invasion: PRESENT.

  F. Left apex -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 9 of 11 mm (82%).

  G. Right lateral base -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 7 of 10 mm (70%).

  H. Right lateral apex -- Benign prostatic tissue. No carcinoma identified.

  I. Left lateral base -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 12 of 12 mm (100%). Perineural invasion: PRESENT.

  J. Left lateral apex -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+4=8. Involved length: 6 of 11 mm (55%).

  K. Right seminal vesicle sampling -- Acinar adenocarcinoma involving seminal vesicle wall. Gleason pattern 5 (sheets and single cells). Seminal vesicle invasion: PRESENT.

  L. Targeted biopsy, left base dominant MRI lesion -- Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9. Involved length: 15.2 of 16 mm (95%). Maximum core involvement across all specimens: 95% (this core). Perineural invasion: PRESENT.

SUMMARY:
  - Cores positive for adenocarcinoma: 10/12 (83%)
  - Maximum percent core involvement: 95% (left base, targeted core)
  - Bilateral lobe involvement: YES (right and left)
  - Predominant Gleason pattern: Pattern 4 (poorly formed glands, fused glands, cribriform architecture)
  - Secondary Gleason pattern: Pattern 5 (individual cells, solid sheets, comedonecrosis)
  - Tertiary Gleason pattern: None (no Gleason pattern 3 identified)
  - Perineural invasion: PRESENT (in 6 of 10 positive cores)
  - Lymphovascular invasion: NOT IDENTIFIED on needle biopsy specimen (see note)
  - Seminal vesicle invasion: PRESENT (core K, direct extension)
  - Intraductal carcinoma of the prostate (IDC-P): PRESENT (cribriform architecture with comedonecrosis in left base and targeted cores, morphologically consistent with intraductal spread; this component is associated with high-grade and aggressive disease)

Note: Absence of lymphovascular invasion on needle biopsy is not exclusionary given the sampling limitations of needle core tissue; the presence of LVI in subsequent surgical or resection specimens cannot be excluded.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES -- IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:

IHC performed on representative positive cores (block D, left base; block L, targeted left base biopsy):

  PSA (prostate-specific antigen): POSITIVE -- diffuse cytoplasmic staining, moderate to strong intensity. Confirms prostatic origin.

  PSMA (prostate-specific membrane antigen): POSITIVE -- strong, diffuse membranous and cytoplasmic staining. Intensity: 3+ across tumor cells.

  NKX3.1 (prostate-specific transcription factor): POSITIVE -- strong nuclear staining in tumor cells. Consistent with acinar adenocarcinoma of prostatic origin.

  AMACR (alpha-methylacyl-CoA racemase, P504S): POSITIVE -- strong granular cytoplasmic staining. Consistent with adenocarcinoma.

  p63 (basal cell marker): NEGATIVE in tumor glands. Absence of basal cell layer confirms invasive acinar adenocarcinoma (no high-grade PIN only).

  CK5/6 (basal cell marker): NEGATIVE in tumor glands. Consistent with invasive adenocarcinoma.

  Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE. No neuroendocrine differentiation.

  Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE. No neuroendocrine differentiation.

  CD56 (NCAM): NEGATIVE. No neuroendocrine differentiation.

  Ki-67 (MIB-1 proliferative index): Approximately 28% of tumor nuclei positive (high proliferative rate, consistent with Grade Group 5 disease).

  ERG (ETS-related gene, TMPRSS2-ERG fusion surrogate): NEGATIVE. No ERG protein overexpression detected. ERG-negative, Gleason 4+5 disease.

Interpretation: The morphological and immunophenotypic profile is diagnostic of high-grade acinar adenocarcinoma of prostatic origin, Gleason score 4+5=9, Grade Group 5. No neuroendocrine differentiation is identified. The ERG-negative status with high Gleason grade is consistent with biologically aggressive disease.

---

ADDITIONAL PATHOLOGICAL FEATURES:

Intraductal Carcinoma of the Prostate (IDC-P): Present. Large cribriform nests with central comedonecrosis are noted within native duct-acinar structures, meeting criteria for IDC-P per ISUP 2019 guidelines. IDC-P is associated with adverse pathological and clinical outcomes and is noted as a separate finding distinct from the invasive tumor grade.

High-grade Prostatic Intraepithelial Neoplasia (HGPIN): Present, focally in uninvolved areas adjacent to tumor.

Benign Prostatic Tissue: Present in 2 of 12 cores (H and partial areas of B), with benign prostatic glands, stroma, and smooth muscle. Benign prostatic hyperplasia-type changes noted.

Inflammation: Focal mild chronic inflammation identified in benign prostatic tissue. No acute prostatitis.

---

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

1. PROSTATE, NEEDLE CORE BIOPSY (12 CORES, TRUS-GUIDED):
   - ACINAR ADENOCARCINOMA OF THE PROSTATE
   - GLEASON SCORE: 4 + 5 = 9
   - GRADE GROUP: 5 (WHO/ISUP 2019)
   - CORES INVOLVED: 10 OF 12 (83%)
   - MAXIMUM CORE INVOLVEMENT: 95% (LEFT BASE, TARGETED CORE)
   - BILATERAL INVOLVEMENT: YES
   - PERINEURAL INVASION: PRESENT
   - LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - SEMINAL VESICLE INVASION: PRESENT (CORE K)
   - INTRADUCTAL CARCINOMA OF THE PROSTATE (IDC-P): PRESENT
   - NO NEUROENDOCRINE DIFFERENTIATION
   - IHC: PSA+, PSMA+, NKX3.1+, AMACR+, p63-, synaptophysin-, chromogranin-, ERG-

---

Pathologist signature: [Attending Surgical Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]

Case reviewed by: [Senior GU Pathologist, MD] (secondary review on high-grade/high-risk case per departmental protocol)

Electronic sign-out: [DATE AND TIME]

Report status: FINAL
